steps.title: Compare treated and untreated
steps.content: The student's conclusion must come from comparing bananas with and without ethylene at each temperature. The graph shows a larger gap between the two treatments at 14°C, 16°C, and 18°C than at 20°C.

steps.title: Choose the gap claim
steps.content: A makes an ideal-storage claim the experiment does not establish. B says temperature differences were not associated with ripening-time differences for untreated bananas, which the graph does not support. C reverses the temperature pattern for ethylene-treated bananas. The answer is D.
